summary: Conjecture 26 of arXiv:2506.08810 proposes an edge-coloured analogue of the paper's main theorem: given any k-colouring c of the edges of a finite clique K using every colour, there should exist a colouring of the complete countably infinite graph such that every locally finite perturbation contains a copy of K with colouring c. The source paper was published in the Canadian Journal of Mathematics (online March 2026) but no follow-up work addressing this specific conjecture was found in an extensive web search.
search_queries: Bonamy Groenland Johnston Morrison Scott "induced-saturated" infinite graphs 2506.08810
"locally finite perturbation" "edge-coloured" "complete countably infinite graph" conjecture
induced-saturated infinite graphs "locally finite perturbation" edge colouring Bonamy 2025 2026
notes: The source paper appeared in the Canadian Journal of Mathematics (published online 2026-03-24). Conjecture 26 is a proposed edge-coloured analogue of the paper's central Theorem 1, stated without attribution to prior work. No follow-up paper resolving or partially addressing this conjecture was found; the conjecture is recent (< 1 year old) and absence of evidence is strong evidence it remains open.
